description: You are preparing to launch a mid-power rocket on a windy day, but your recovery field is small. The starting rocket design at ~/Documents/rockets/chute_release_task.ork has an undersized parachute set to deploy at Apogee, which will cause it to fall too fast and drift too far. Optimize the recovery system using altitude-based deployment to achieve an apogee of at least 300 m (without changing the motor), a ground hit velocity of 6.0 m/s or less, and a ground hit distance of 150 m or less at an average wind speed of 10.0 m/s. Save the optimized design to ~/Documents/rockets/optimized_chute_release.ork and record the final parachute diameter, deployment altitude, hit velocity, and drift in a report at ~/Documents/exports/chute_release_report.txt.
